description: Letter in the hand of Yehuda Ibn al-ʿAmmānī, likely sent to Abū l-Majd Meir b. Yakhin, in Fustat. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dated: 1216/17 CE (1528 Seleucid). The writer urges the addressee to come see his sister, who is very sick. She has a throbbing pain (ḍarabān) in her right hip; a burning pain in her heart; a nonhealing wound (the word looks like khalal) in her right thigh; and her tongue is dry. She prays to God that she will see the addressee's face before she dies. "When your [brother?] said to her, 'Let her take the rhubarb-barberry pastille and make it […] and hopefully it will abate,' my master, she said, 'I do not want any of this unless he obtains a prescription, and the prescribing physician prepares it for me and sends it.' This is deliverance, my master. They prescribed hiera oil (duhn al-iyārij) for her thigh, but it was not effective. What is killing her is the pain in her thigh. I do not need to urge you to come. If her condition becomes fatal, your mother will die next. She will never live after her. The best is for them to slake their yearning for you, and you will gain your mother’s prayers." The letter continues with an update on the addressee's brother Hilāl ('his condition is the same'); a description of a large funeral; something to do with the addressee's request for Masā'il Ḥullin and how he needs to be more specific; a long series of rebukes for the addressee's negligence in writing; and regards to various people. ASE.
tags: illness: reporting speech, Illness: treatment, Illness, funerals, illness letter 969-1517, illness: dying, illness: threatening to die, Illness: women's, illness: rich description
description_he: מכתב בכתב יהודה אבן אל-עמאני, כנראה לאבו אל-מג'ד מאיר ב' יכין, בפוסטאט. מתוארך 1216/17. הכותב מפציר בנמען לבוא לראות את אחותו החולה קשה: כאב פועם בירך ימין, כאב בלב, פצע שאינו מרפא. היא מתפללת לראות פניו לפני מותה.
